title: How make background only fit in S+B model

posts.name: Alexandre Nikitenko
posts.text:
Dear experts,
with this command:
combine -M MultiDimFit -d combined.txt --redefineSignalPOIs mu_sr1_2017,mu_sr2_2017,mu_sr3_2017 --algo fixed --fixedPointPOIs mu_sr1_2017=0,mu_sr2_2017=0,mu_sr3_2017=0 --setParameters mean=27.3,width=3.2 --freezeParameters mean,width,r -v3
I do in first pass S+B fit and  in second pass B only fit.
How should I modify it to make background only fit ?  Clearly I should remove --algo fixed and  --fixedPointPOI mu_sr1_2017=0,mu_sr2_2017=0,mu_sr3_2017=0
but how I should setup zero all signal strengths ?
I have tried
combine -M MultiDimFit -d combined.txt --redefineSignalPOIs mu_sr1_2017,mu_sr2_2017,mu_sr3_2017 --setParameters mean=27.3,width=3.2,mu_sr1_2017=0,mu_sr2_2017=0,mu_sr3_2017=0 --freezeParameters mean,width,mu_sr1_2017=0,mu_sr2_2017=0,mu_sr3_2017=0,r -v3
but it still makes S+B fit and reports
best fit parameter values:
mu_sr1_2017 :   +40.510
mu_sr2_2017 :   +25.891
mu_sr3_2017 :   +58.530
and at the beginning said:
Redefining the POIs to be: RooArgSet:: = (mu_sr1_2017,mu_sr2_2017,mu_sr3_2017)
WARNING: cannot freeze nuisance parameter mu_sr1_2017=0 if it doesn’t exist!
WARNING: cannot freeze nuisance parameter mu_sr2_2017=0 if it doesn’t exist!
WARNING: cannot freeze nuisance parameter mu_sr3_2017=0 if it doesn’t exist!
Please, help.
Thanks, Sasha.
posts.cooked_html:
Dear experts,

with this command:

combine -M MultiDimFit -d combined.txt --redefineSignalPOIs mu_sr1_2017,mu_sr2_2017,mu_sr3_2017 --algo fixed --fixedPointPOIs mu_sr1_2017=0,mu_sr2_2017=0,mu_sr3_2017=0 --setParameters mean=27.3,width=3.2 --freezeParameters mean,width,r -v3

I do in first pass S+B fit and in second pass B only fit.

How should I modify it to make background only fit ? Clearly I should remove --algo fixed and --fixedPointPOI mu_sr1_2017=0,mu_sr2_2017=0,mu_sr3_2017=0

but how I should setup zero all signal strengths ?

I have tried

combine -M MultiDimFit -d combined.txt --redefineSignalPOIs mu_sr1_2017,mu_sr2_2017,mu_sr3_2017 --setParameters mean=27.3,width=3.2,mu_sr1_2017=0,mu_sr2_2017=0,mu_sr3_2017=0 --freezeParameters mean,width,mu_sr1_2017=0,mu_sr2_2017=0,mu_sr3_2017=0,r -v3

but it still makes S+B fit and reports

best fit parameter values:
mu_sr1_2017 : +40.510
mu_sr2_2017 : +25.891
mu_sr3_2017 : +58.530

and at the beginning said:

Redefining the POIs to be: RooArgSet:: = (mu_sr1_2017,mu_sr2_2017,mu_sr3_2017)
WARNING: cannot freeze nuisance parameter mu_sr1_2017=0 if it doesn’t exist!
WARNING: cannot freeze nuisance parameter mu_sr2_2017=0 if it doesn’t exist!
WARNING: cannot freeze nuisance parameter mu_sr3_2017=0 if it doesn’t exist!

Please, help.

Thanks, Sasha.

posts.name: Philip Keicher
posts.text:
Hi Sasha,
The feature that you observe is because you have included the =0 in your --freezeParameters option - this option should only contain the names of the parameters you want to fix, not any values. Changing your command above to
combine -M MultiDimFit -d combined.txt \
--redefineSignalPOIs mu_sr1_2017,mu_sr2_2017,mu_sr3_2017 \
--setParameters mean=27.3,width=3.2,mu_sr1_2017=0,mu_sr2_2017=0,mu_sr3_2017=0 \
--freezeParameters mean,width,mu_sr1_2017,mu_sr2_2017,mu_sr3_2017,r -v3

should do the trick.
That being said, I’m not sure I understand what you want to do. --algo fixed evaluates the post-fit NLL at a certain point. Is this what you want to do? Or do you want to compare the NLL you obtain from a b-only fit with the s+b NLL at a given set of parameters?
Side note: you can use

--saveFitResult to save a RooFitResult object that contains the hessian and post-fit values of your parameters
--saveWorkspace option to save the post-fit workspace. You can load the post-fit workspace using the corresponding snap shot, see the docs here.

Hope this helps! Cheers,
Philip
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Sasha,

The feature that you observe is because you have included the =0 in your --freezeParameters option - this option should only contain the names of the parameters you want to fix, not any values. Changing your command above to

combine -M MultiDimFit -d combined.txt \
  --redefineSignalPOIs mu_sr1_2017,mu_sr2_2017,mu_sr3_2017 \
  --setParameters mean=27.3,width=3.2,mu_sr1_2017=0,mu_sr2_2017=0,mu_sr3_2017=0 \
  --freezeParameters mean,width,mu_sr1_2017,mu_sr2_2017,mu_sr3_2017,r -v3

should do the trick.

That being said, I’m not sure I understand what you want to do. --algo fixed evaluates the post-fit NLL at a certain point. Is this what you want to do? Or do you want to compare the NLL you obtain from a b-only fit with the s+b NLL at a given set of parameters?

Side note: you can use

  • --saveFitResult to save a RooFitResult object that contains the hessian and post-fit values of your parameters
  • --saveWorkspace option to save the post-fit workspace. You can load the post-fit workspace using the corresponding snap shot, see the docs here.

Hope this helps! Cheers,
Philip

posts.name: Alexandre Nikitenko
posts.text:
Hi Philip,
Thank you, it works !
Actually I want to do separately S+B fit  and then B only fit.  In both separate fits I have my initial parameters for bkg functions as input.
And I would like if possible to get two NLL for these two fits.
My problem is that when I do combine with --algo fixed  it does first fit S+B
and for the second B-only fit it gets initial values not from me, but from the first fit.  And some time due to this I got  into local minimum in B-only fit.
Thanks, Sasha.
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Philip,

Thank you, it works !

Actually I want to do separately S+B fit and then B only fit. In both separate fits I have my initial parameters for bkg functions as input.
And I would like if possible to get two NLL for these two fits.

My problem is that when I do combine with --algo fixed it does first fit S+B
and for the second B-only fit it gets initial values not from me, but from the first fit. And some time due to this I got into local minimum in B-only fit.

Thanks, Sasha.
